metadata.note: Output used as-is without manual revision or selection.

causal_texts: The glass slipped from Maria's hand. The glass fell to the floor. The glass broke into many pieces. Maria swept the floor carefully.

Daniel filled the metal kettle with cold water from the kitchen sink. Daniel placed the metal kettle on the stove above a blue flame. Daniel heard the metal kettle whistle beside the simmering soup pot. Daniel poured hot water from the metal kettle into a ceramic mug.

Nina found the bicycle tire flat beside the apartment entrance. Nina attached the hand pump to the bicycle tire valve. Nina pressed the hand pump until the bicycle tire felt firm. Nina rode the bicycle along the quiet street toward the market.

Omar opened the paint can beside the wooden garden fence. Omar dipped the wide brush into the paint can carefully. Omar spread blue paint across the wooden garden fence slowly. Omar washed the wide brush under the outdoor tap.

Priya dug a small hole in the vegetable garden bed. Priya placed the tomato seedling into the small hole gently. Priya covered the tomato seedling roots with dark soil. Priya watered the tomato seedling with a green watering can.

Elena mixed flour and sugar inside the large glass bowl. Elena added eggs and butter to the large glass bowl. Elena poured batter from the large glass bowl into a pan. Elena removed a golden cake from the warm oven.

Victor spread the wrinkled shirt across the ironing board. Victor plugged the hot iron into the wall socket. Victor pressed the hot iron over the wrinkled shirt slowly. Victor hung the wrinkled shirt inside the bedroom closet.

Rosa placed the parcel on the post office counter. Rosa wrote the address on the parcel with black ink. Rosa paid for postage on the parcel at the register. Rosa dropped the parcel into the outgoing mail bin.

Kenji opened the dusty window above the study desk. Kenji sprayed cleaner across the dusty window with steady hands. Kenji wiped the dusty window using a folded cotton cloth. Kenji closed the clean window before the evening breeze.

Amina dropped the blue notebook into a puddle near school. Amina lifted the blue notebook from the puddle quickly. Amina dried the blue notebook under the classroom fan. Amina copied fresh notes into the blue notebook carefully.

Carlos turned the brass key inside the front door lock. Carlos pushed the front door open with a shoulder. Carlos carried the grocery bag through the front door carefully. Carlos placed the grocery bag on the kitchen counter.

Leah placed the glass vase on the dining room table. Leah filled the glass vase with water from the tap. Leah arranged fresh tulips inside the glass vase neatly. Leah admired the bright tulips beside the sunny window.

Mateo unfolded the camping tent on the grassy field. Mateo pushed the tent poles through the camping tent sleeves. Mateo raised the camping tent beside the riverbank firmly. Mateo zipped the camping tent before the night rain.

Hana loaded dirty plates into the dishwasher after dinner. Hana added soap to the dishwasher compartment near the latch. Hana pressed the dishwasher button beside the kitchen timer. Hana unloaded clean plates from the dishwasher after breakfast.

Felix placed the blank canvas on the studio easel. Felix squeezed red paint onto the wooden palette carefully. Felix brushed red paint across the blank canvas in wide strokes. Felix leaned the painted canvas against the brick wall.

Isabel tucked the white sheet over the mattress corners. Isabel placed the soft blanket across the white sheet smoothly. Isabel arranged the pillows above the soft blanket neatly. Isabel closed the bedroom door beside the tidy bed.

Jonas opened the toolbox beside the garage workbench. Jonas selected the small screwdriver from the toolbox carefully. Jonas tightened the loose hinge on the cabinet door. Jonas closed the toolbox after the cabinet door moved quietly.

Lina carried the wicker basket into the strawberry field. Lina picked ripe berries into the wicker basket carefully. Lina brought the wicker basket home filled with ripe berries. Lina washed the ripe berries in a metal colander.

Marcus opened the violin case near the concert chair. Marcus tightened the bow hair on the violin bow slowly. Marcus drew the violin bow across the violin strings smoothly. Marcus returned the violin bow to the violin case.

Naomi set the alarm clock for six in the morning. Naomi heard the alarm clock ring beside the lamp. Naomi turned off the alarm clock with a sleepy hand. Naomi left the bedroom before sunrise touched the curtains.

Diego placed the soccer ball near the goal line. Diego stepped toward the soccer ball with quick feet. Diego kicked the soccer ball past the diving goalkeeper. Diego celebrated the goal with a wide smile.

Clara filled the bird feeder with sunflower seeds before noon. Clara hung the bird feeder from the maple branch. Clara watched a bright finch land on the bird feeder. Clara smiled beside the window with warm tea.

Yusuf folded the letter inside a blue envelope. Yusuf sealed the blue envelope with careful pressure. Yusuf carried the blue envelope to the corner mailbox. Yusuf slid the blue envelope through the narrow metal slot.

Sofia placed the chessboard on the wooden coffee table. Sofia arranged the chess pieces across the chessboard carefully. Sofia moved the white queen across the chessboard decisively. Sofia announced checkmate with a quiet laugh.

Arun opened the refrigerator after the afternoon grocery trip. Arun placed fresh vegetables inside the refrigerator on the middle shelf. Arun closed the refrigerator before warm air filled the kitchen. Arun cooked fresh vegetables in a large pan.

Bianca rolled the suitcase across the airport floor. Bianca placed the suitcase on the baggage scale. Bianca checked the suitcase at the airline counter. Bianca walked toward security with a boarding pass.

Tomás tied the apron around the waist. Tomás sliced the red onion on the cutting board. Tomás dropped the red onion into the hot skillet. Tomás stirred the red onion until a sweet smell filled the kitchen.

Greta opened the sketchbook beside the sunny classroom window. Greta drew a tall lighthouse inside the sketchbook carefully. Greta shaded the tall lighthouse with a soft pencil. Greta showed the sketchbook to the art teacher proudly.

Jamal put the stack of clothes into the washing machine. Jamal poured detergent into the washing machine drawer. Jamal started the washing machine with a firm button press. Jamal hung the clean clothes on the balcony line.

Mei placed the tea leaves into the porcelain teapot. Mei poured hot water into the porcelain teapot slowly. Mei waited while the tea leaves colored the water. Mei served fragrant tea from the porcelain teapot into cups.

Otto took the library book from the canvas bag. Otto opened the library book under the reading lamp. Otto turned pages in the library book until midnight. Otto returned the library book before the due date.

Farah set the ladder against the apple tree trunk. Farah climbed the ladder with the wicker basket. Farah picked red apples into the wicker basket carefully. Farah carried the wicker basket home filled with red apples.
Riku placed the snow boots beside the front mat. Riku pulled the snow boots onto cold feet. Riku walked through deep snow in the snow boots. Riku left the snow boots near the heater grate.

Helena fastened the silver necklace around the neck. Helena looked at the silver necklace in the mirror. Helena wore the silver necklace to the evening banquet. Helena returned the silver necklace to the velvet box.
Samir unrolled the yoga mat across the wooden floor. Samir stepped onto the yoga mat with bare feet. Samir held a balanced pose on the yoga mat quietly. Samir rolled the yoga mat beside the wall.

Lucia placed the candle inside the glass lantern. Lucia lit the candle inside the glass lantern carefully. Lucia carried the glass lantern along the dark garden path. Lucia set the glass lantern beside the stone bench.

Peter pushed the lawn mower across the tall backyard grass. Peter emptied clippings from the lawn mower bag twice. Peter swept stray clippings from the stone path. Peter parked the lawn mower inside the garden shed.

Anika opened the jar of strawberry jam. Anika spread strawberry jam across the toasted bread evenly. Anika placed the toasted bread on a white plate. Anika ate the toasted bread beside the kitchen window.

Noah unlocked the bicycle chain near the school gate. Noah mounted the bicycle beside the empty sidewalk. Noah pedaled the bicycle through the cool morning air. Noah locked the bicycle outside the office building.
Sara picked the seashell from wet sand. Sara rinsed the seashell in shallow seawater gently. Sara carried the seashell home inside a coat pocket. Sara placed the seashell on the bedroom shelf.

Emil opened the notebook during the history lecture. Emil wrote careful notes inside the notebook for an hour. Emil underlined important dates inside the notebook with blue ink. Emil reviewed the notebook at the library table.

Layla placed the cereal bowl under the cereal box. Layla poured oats into the cereal bowl carefully. Layla added milk to the cereal bowl from a carton. Layla carried the cereal bowl to the breakfast table.

Pavel pushed the elevator button in the hotel lobby. Pavel entered the elevator after the sliding doors opened. Pavel pressed the seventh floor button inside the elevator. Pavel stepped from the elevator into a quiet hallway.

Chloe opened the freezer above the lower cabinet. Chloe removed the ice tray from the freezer shelf. Chloe twisted the ice tray over the metal bowl. Chloe dropped clear cubes from the ice tray into lemonade.

Haruto spread the map across the picnic table. Haruto traced the trail on the map with a finger. Haruto followed the trail beyond the waterfall overlook. Haruto folded the map beside the mountain cabin.
Mira placed the paper form on the office desk. Mira wrote the address on the paper form neatly. Mira signed the paper form above the printed line. Mira handed the paper form to the receptionist.

Ivan set the dominoes in a long curving line. Ivan tapped the first domino with a careful finger. Ivan watched the dominoes fall across the tabletop rapidly. Ivan smiled at the final domino near the lamp.

Eva opened the wardrobe before the morning meeting. Eva selected the blue jacket from the wardrobe carefully. Eva buttoned the blue jacket in front of the mirror. Eva left the apartment wearing the blue jacket.
Kai carried the toolbox to the loose chair. Kai examined the loose chair beside the kitchen table. Kai tightened the loose chair with a small wrench. Kai tested the loose chair with steady pressure.

Nora placed the loaf of bread on the cutting board. Nora sliced the loaf of bread with a serrated knife. Nora arranged warm slices from the loaf of bread in a basket. Nora served the loaf of bread beside vegetable soup.

Ahmed unlocked the classroom cabinet with a brass key. Ahmed removed the projector from the classroom cabinet carefully. Ahmed connected the projector to the laptop with a cable. Ahmed dimmed the room before the presentation started.
Julia planted the paper label beside the herb pot. Julia watered the herb pot near the balcony rail. Julia trimmed leaves from the herb pot for dinner. Julia moved the herb pot away from strong wind.

Ben carried the stack of plates from the cupboard. Ben placed the stack of plates on the dining table. Ben added forks beside the stack of plates carefully. Ben invited the guests toward the dining table.

Yara folded the picnic blanket beside the lake shore. Yara placed the picnic blanket into the wicker basket. Yara carried the wicker basket toward the parking lot. Yara loaded the wicker basket into the car trunk.

Leo inserted the coin into the vending machine slot. Leo pressed the orange button on the vending machine. Leo caught the cold bottle from the vending machine tray. Leo opened the cold bottle beside the station bench.
Tina untied the ribbon around the gift box. Tina lifted the lid from the gift box slowly. Tina found a silver bracelet inside the gift box. Tina clasped the silver bracelet around the wrist.

Rafael opened the camera bag near the fountain. Rafael attached the lens to the camera body carefully. Rafael pointed the camera toward the sunset clouds. Rafael placed the camera back into the camera bag.

Mai placed the metal bucket beneath the leaking pipe. Mai watched the metal bucket collect steady drops of water. Mai called the plumber about the leaking pipe. Mai emptied the metal bucket into the sink.

Ethan spread the puzzle pieces across the living room rug. Ethan found the corner pieces among the puzzle pieces quickly. Ethan connected the puzzle pieces into a bright picture. Ethan placed the finished puzzle on the coffee table.

Alma opened the piano bench before the lesson. Alma removed the music book from the piano bench storage. Alma placed the music book on the piano stand. Alma played scales from the music book with patient hands.

Vikram hung the wet towel on the balcony rail. Vikram watched the wet towel move in the afternoon wind. Vikram checked the wet towel after bright sunlight faded. Vikram carried the dry towel into the bathroom.

Mina placed the shopping list on the supermarket cart handle. Mina followed the shopping list through crowded aisles carefully. Mina crossed bread from the shopping list with a pencil. Mina paid for groceries beside the exit doors.

Oscar poured birdseed into the metal scoop. Oscar emptied the metal scoop into the chicken trough. Oscar watched the chickens gather around the chicken trough. Oscar closed the gate near the chicken trough.
Rina placed the silver ring on the bathroom sink. Rina washed soap from the silver ring under warm water. Rina dried the silver ring with a soft towel. Rina slid the silver ring onto a finger.

Darius opened the curtain before the afternoon rehearsal. Darius raised the curtain above the empty stage slowly. Darius welcomed the audience from the center mark. Darius lowered the curtain after the final bow.

Yuki put the notebook computer on the wooden desk. Yuki connected the notebook computer to the power cable. Yuki opened the notebook computer before the online meeting. Yuki typed notes on the notebook computer during discussion.

Nadia tied the running shoes beside the park bench. Nadia stretched beside the park bench under cool shade. Nadia ran two laps around the quiet pond. Nadia untied the running shoes near the water fountain.

Bruno placed the charcoal pieces inside the metal grill. Bruno lit the charcoal pieces with a long match. Bruno arranged corn on the metal grill carefully. Bruno turned the corn above the glowing charcoal pieces.

Aiko opened the drawer beneath the bathroom mirror. Aiko removed the hairbrush from the bathroom drawer gently. Aiko brushed long hair with the hairbrush before school. Aiko returned the hairbrush to the bathroom drawer.
Felipe placed the clay ball on the spinning wheel. Felipe shaped the clay ball with wet hands. Felipe lifted a narrow vase from the spinning wheel. Felipe placed the narrow vase on the drying shelf.

Zara opened the umbrella during the sudden street shower. Zara held the umbrella above the wool coat firmly. Zara crossed the plaza beneath the umbrella carefully. Zara closed the umbrella inside the café doorway.

Marek put the coins into the parking meter slot. Marek checked the parking meter screen for remaining minutes. Marek walked away from the parking meter toward the bakery. Marek returned before the parking meter expired.
Ruth set the alarm on the small kitchen timer. Ruth kneaded the bread dough on the floured counter. Ruth heard the small kitchen timer ring near the oven. Ruth placed the bread dough into a loaf pan.

Keiko spread the newspaper across the coffee table. Keiko circled job listings in the newspaper with red pen. Keiko copied phone numbers from the newspaper onto a pad. Keiko folded the newspaper beside the sofa arm.

Martin opened the toolbox after the bicycle chain slipped. Martin lifted the bicycle chain onto the rear gear carefully. Martin turned the pedals until the bicycle chain moved smoothly. Martin closed the toolbox beside the driveway.

Sana pulled the curtain aside before sunrise reached the room. Sana opened the curtain across the bedroom window fully. Sana watched pink clouds through the bedroom window quietly. Sana closed the curtain before the midday heat.

Julian placed the soccer jersey on the locker room bench. Julian pulled the soccer jersey over the shoulders quickly. Julian ran onto the field wearing the soccer jersey. Julian hung the soccer jersey after the muddy match.

Elif opened the spice jar above the stove. Elif sprinkled cumin from the spice jar into the soup. Elif stirred the soup until warm steam filled the kitchen. Elif closed the spice jar beside the cutting board.

Mateusz carried the watering hose across the backyard lawn. Mateusz connected the watering hose to the outdoor faucet. Mateusz sprayed the rose bushes with the watering hose slowly. Mateusz coiled the watering hose beside the garage wall.

Aya took the train ticket from the coat pocket. Aya inserted the train ticket into the station gate. Aya walked through the station gate toward platform three. Aya kept the train ticket inside the wallet for the return trip.

Sebastian set the compass on the hiking map. Sebastian checked the compass beside a tall pine tree. Sebastian followed the compass toward the northern ridge. Sebastian packed the compass into the backpack before dusk.
Ines placed the soap bar near the bathroom faucet. Ines washed the paintbrush with the soap bar carefully. Ines rinsed the paintbrush under warm water. Ines rested the clean paintbrush on the towel.
David opened the can of beans with a steel opener. David poured the beans from the can into a pot. David heated the beans on the stove over medium flame. David served the beans beside toasted bread.
Eri spread the beach towel on warm sand. Eri placed the sunglasses on the beach towel carefully. Eri rested on the beach towel under bright sun. Eri shook sand from the beach towel before leaving.

Karim placed the stamp album on the desk lamp side. Karim opened the stamp album to an empty page. Karim fixed a new stamp inside the stamp album carefully. Karim closed the stamp album with a satisfied smile.

Lena unfolded the raincoat beside the apartment doorway. Lena buttoned the raincoat against the gray weather quickly. Lena walked to the bus stop wearing the raincoat. Lena hung the raincoat on the hallway hook.

Hugo placed the tennis racket near the court fence. Hugo gripped the tennis racket before the first serve. Hugo swung the tennis racket through the yellow ball. Hugo leaned the tennis racket against the bench near the scoreboard.

Iris set the candle mold on the kitchen tray. Iris poured warm wax into the candle mold slowly. Iris waited while the candle mold cooled near the window. Iris removed a white candle from the candle mold.
Niko picked the fallen branch from the garden path. Niko carried the fallen branch to the compost pile. Niko raked leaves around the compost pile carefully. Niko returned the rake to the garden shed.

Selma placed the passport on the airport counter. Selma handed the passport to the border officer calmly. Selma received the passport beside the stamped boarding card. Selma walked toward the departure gate with relief.

Adrian pulled the curtains around the small stage. Adrian checked the microphone on the small stage carefully. Adrian spoke into the microphone before the audience arrived. Adrian welcomed the audience from the small stage.

Maya opened the sewing kit near the armchair lamp. Maya threaded the needle from the sewing kit carefully. Maya stitched the loose button onto the coat. Maya closed the sewing kit after the coat looked neat.

Rohan set the chess clock beside the tournament board. Rohan pressed the chess clock after the opening move. Rohan watched the chess clock during a tense ending. Rohan stopped the chess clock after a handshake.

Elena carried the glass pitcher to the kitchen sink. Elena filled the glass pitcher with cold lemonade. Elena brought the glass pitcher back filled with cold lemonade. Elena poured lemonade from the glass pitcher into glasses.

Boris placed the firewood beside the brick fireplace. Boris stacked the firewood inside the brick fireplace carefully. Boris lit the firewood with a paper twist. Boris watched orange flames rise from the brick fireplace.

Naoko put the math workbook on the study table. Naoko solved ten problems in the math workbook carefully. Naoko checked answers in the math workbook with a ruler. Naoko closed the math workbook before dinner.

Celine opened the jewelry box on the dresser top. Celine lifted the pearl earrings from the jewelry box gently. Celine wore the pearl earrings to the family dinner. Celine returned the pearl earrings to the jewelry box.

Artur spread the picnic map across the car hood. Artur marked the river bridge on the picnic map. Artur drove toward the river bridge along a narrow road. Artur folded the picnic map after reaching the meadow.
Mika placed the soup bowl on the microwave plate. Mika heated the soup bowl for two minutes. Mika carried the soup bowl to the table carefully. Mika ate warm soup from the soup bowl slowly.

Tariq opened the closet near the front hallway. Tariq selected the black umbrella from the closet quickly. Tariq carried the black umbrella into steady rain. Tariq returned the black umbrella to the closet near evening.
noncausal_texts: A violin is played with a bow. A flute is played by blowing air. A drum is played by striking the drumhead. A harp is played by plucking strings.
An apron protects clothing during cooking. A whisk mixes eggs and cream quickly. A colander drains water from cooked pasta. A grater shreds cheese into thin pieces.

A tulip grows from a bulb beneath garden soil. A sunflower turns toward bright sunlight during growth. A lily produces large flowers with a strong fragrance. A rose bush often carries sharp thorns on the stems.
An oak tree produces acorns in autumn forests. A pine tree keeps green needles through cold winters. A willow tree grows well near rivers and ponds. A maple tree can provide sweet sap for syrup.
A penguin swims with strong flippers in cold oceans. A sparrow builds small nests near human homes. A heron stands quietly in shallow water for fish. A parrot can mimic sounds with a curved beak.

An octopus has eight arms and remarkable problem-solving ability. A dolphin breathes air through an opening above the head. A starfish moves with tube feet on the seafloor. A jellyfish drifts with ocean currents and trailing tentacles.
A cow provides milk on many farms. A sheep grows thick wool for warm clothing. A goat climbs steep ground with strong balance. A chicken lays eggs in a nesting box.

A butterfly drinks nectar with a long coiled mouthpart. A beetle carries a hard shell over the wings. A dragonfly has large eyes and two pairs of wings. An ant lives in organized colonies under soil or wood.
A turtle carries a hard shell on the back. A snake moves without legs across sand or grass. A lizard can bask on warm rocks for heat. A crocodile waits near riverbanks with powerful jaws.
A frog begins life in water as a tadpole. A salamander often lives under logs in damp forests. A toad has dry skin and a broad body. A newt can return to ponds during breeding season.
A horse can run fast across open fields. A bat flies at night and uses echolocation. A bear has strong claws for digging and climbing. A rabbit has long ears and quick jumping legs.
An apple grows on orchard trees in temperate climates. A banana grows in clusters on a tall plant. A grape grows in bunches on climbing vines. An orange has a thick peel and juicy segments.

A carrot grows underground and contains orange pigment. A spinach plant produces soft edible green leaves. A pumpkin grows on sprawling vines near warm soil. A cucumber develops from yellow flowers on creeping stems.

Rice feeds large populations across Asia and many countries worldwide. Oats are often cooked into porridge for breakfast meals. Corn grows on tall stalks with broad leaves. Barley is used in soup, bread, and malt.
Cinnamon comes from the inner bark of certain trees. Pepper adds sharp heat to many savory dishes. Basil has fragrant leaves for salads and sauces. Turmeric gives food a bright yellow color.
Ice cream is served cold in bowls or cones. Brownies are baked bars with rich chocolate flavor. Pudding has a soft texture and a spoonable form. Pie usually has a crust around sweet filling.
A baguette has a long shape and a crisp crust. A bagel has a round shape with a center hole. A tortilla is flat and flexible for wrapping food. A croissant has many flaky layers of buttery dough.
Cheddar has a firm texture and a sharp flavor. Mozzarella melts smoothly on pizza and baked dishes. Brie has a soft center beneath a pale rind. Parmesan is often grated over pasta and soup.
Tea is made by steeping leaves in hot water. Coffee is brewed from roasted and ground beans. Lemonade combines lemon juice, water, and sugar. Cocoa is prepared from chocolate powder and warm milk.

Cotton feels soft and works well in summer clothing. Wool keeps warmth in coats, sweaters, and scarves. Silk has a smooth surface and a natural sheen. Linen comes from flax fibers and wrinkles easily.
Ruby has a deep red color and strong hardness. Sapphire often appears blue in rings and necklaces. Emerald shows a rich green color in fine jewelry. Opal can display shifting flashes of many colors.

Gold resists corrosion and shines with a warm luster. Iron is used in tools, bridges, and machines. Copper conducts electricity in wires and electronic parts. Aluminum is light and useful in cans and aircraft.

Granite contains quartz, feldspar, and mica crystals. Marble forms from limestone under heat and pressure. Basalt cools from lava and often appears dark. Sandstone is made from compacted grains of sand.

A thermometer measures temperature in air, water, or food. A barometer measures pressure in the atmosphere. A compass points toward magnetic north on Earth. A microscope magnifies very small objects for close study.

A stapler joins papers with small metal fasteners. A binder keeps documents together inside a single cover. A ruler measures length with marked units. A highlighter marks important words with bright color.

A sofa provides comfortable seating in many living rooms. A bookshelf holds novels, manuals, and reference volumes. A wardrobe stores clothing behind tall doors. A desk offers a flat surface for writing and work.

A bicycle moves with pedals, wheels, and human power. A subway train carries many passengers through city tunnels. A sailboat moves with wind across open water. A helicopter can hover above a small landing area.
A basketball is bounced and thrown toward a high hoop. A baseball is hit with a bat on a field. A volleyball is struck over a net by hand. A tennis ball is hit with a racket across a court.
A scarf wraps around the neck in cold weather. A raincoat keeps clothing dry during wet weather. A swimsuit is worn for swimming in pools or seas. A sweater provides warmth during cool evenings.
A sandal leaves much of the foot uncovered. A boot covers the ankle and often resists water. A slipper is worn indoors for comfort and warmth. A sneaker has a flexible sole for walking or running.
A beanie fits closely over the head in cold weather. A helmet protects the head during risky activities. A sun hat provides shade for the face and neck. A beret has a soft round shape without a brim.
A hammer drives nails into wood and soft materials. A saw cuts boards with sharp teeth. A wrench turns nuts and bolts on machinery. A chisel shapes wood or stone with a sharp edge.
A trowel moves soil in flower beds and garden pots. A rake gathers leaves across lawns and paths. Pruning shears cut thin branches on shrubs and vines. A hoe breaks soil and removes young weeds.
A broom sweeps dust from floors and porches. A sponge absorbs water on sinks and tiles. A mop cleans large floors with water and soap. A dustpan collects dirt after sweeping.
A bucket carries water, sand, or tools easily. A bottle stores liquids behind a narrow opening. A basket holds fruit, bread, or laundry. A jar often stores jam, spices, or dry beans.
A lantern gives portable light during camping trips. A chandelier hangs from a ceiling above a room. A flashlight shines a narrow beam in dark places. A desk lamp directs light onto books and papers.

A suspension bridge hangs from cables between tall towers. A stone arch bridge supports weight through curved masonry. A beam bridge spans short distances with straight supports. A drawbridge can rise for passing boats.

A canoe moves quietly through lakes and slow rivers. A ferry carries passengers across a body of water. A kayak has a narrow shape for paddling. A cargo ship transports goods across long ocean routes.
A jet climbs quickly and travels at high speed. A glider stays aloft without an engine for long periods. A helicopter rises vertically above the ground. A hot air balloon floats with heated air.
Mercury circles the Sun in a short orbital period. Neptune is a cold giant in the outer Solar System. Mars has dusty plains and tall volcanoes. Venus has a dense atmosphere and intense surface heat.

Fog reduces visibility near roads, fields, and coastlines. Hail falls as hard ice during strong storms. A drought brings long periods with very little rain. Thunder travels through air after a lightning flash.
A cactus stores water inside thick green stems. A date palm grows in hot dry regions. An agave forms heavy leaves around a central core. A Joshua tree grows in deserts of the American Southwest.
A moose has long legs for movement through marshy forests. A lynx has tufted ears and silent steps. A wolf lives in packs across large territories. An owl hunts small animals during darkness.
A polar bear lives on sea ice in the Arctic. A walrus has long tusks and thick blubber. An arctic fox grows a white coat in winter. A seal swims efficiently in very cold water.

A toucan has a large colorful bill in rainforest canopies. A jaguar moves quietly through dense tropical forests. A poison dart frog shows bright warning colors on skin. A sloth hangs from branches with curved claws.
A salmon can swim upstream during spawning runs. A trout often lives in cool clear streams. A catfish has whisker-like barbels near the mouth. A carp feeds near lake bottoms and muddy ponds.
A tuna swims fast through open ocean waters. A clownfish lives among sea anemone tentacles. A swordfish has a long pointed upper jaw. A seahorse swims upright with a curled tail.

A lavender plant produces fragrant purple flower spikes. A mint plant spreads quickly through garden beds. A rosemary plant has narrow leaves with strong aroma. A parsley plant provides fresh green leaves for cooking.
An almond grows inside a hard shell on a tree. A walnut has a wrinkled kernel inside a round shell. A peanut develops underground on the roots. A pistachio often has a partly opened shell.
A pumpkin seed can be roasted for a crunchy snack. A sesame seed is small and rich in oil. A sunflower seed comes from the center of a large flower. A flaxseed is used in bread and cereal.
Pancakes are cooked on a flat griddle surface. Yogurt has a tangy flavor and creamy texture. Toast is made by browning slices of bread. Granola combines oats, nuts, and sweetener in clusters.
Spaghetti forms long thin strands for sauce. Penne has short tubes with angled ends. Fusilli has a spiral shape with good sauce coverage. Lasagna comes in wide flat sheets for layered dishes.
Brick forms strong walls in many buildings. Concrete hardens from cement, water, and aggregate. Glass admits light through windows and doors. Tile covers floors and roofs with durable pieces.
A kitchen is used for cooking and food storage. A bedroom is used for sleeping and rest. A library is used for reading and quiet study. A garage is used for vehicles and storage.

A notebook holds handwritten notes on bound pages. A folder carries loose papers between places. An envelope holds letters and documents for mailing. A calendar shows dates, months, and scheduled events.
A clock shows time with hands or digits. A sundial uses sunlight and a shadow for time. An hourglass measures duration with falling sand. A stopwatch records short intervals with precise timing.

Solar panels convert sunlight into electrical power. Wind turbines convert moving air into electricity. Hydroelectric dams use flowing water for energy production. Geothermal plants use underground heat for power generation.
A canyon has steep walls carved by water or erosion. A plateau rises high above surrounding land. A dune forms from sand moved by wind. A delta forms at the mouth of a river.
A bay curves inward along a coastline. A reef grows from coral in warm shallow seas. A glacier moves slowly across land as compacted ice. A waterfall drops water over a steep rock edge.

A heart pumps blood through arteries and veins. A lung exchanges oxygen and carbon dioxide during breathing. A liver processes nutrients and removes waste products. A kidney filters blood and produces urine.
An eye detects light and color from the surroundings. An ear detects sound and helps balance the body. A nose detects odors in air. A tongue detects sweet, sour, salty, and bitter tastes.
A blender mixes fruit and liquid into smooth drinks. A toaster browns slices of bread with heat. A microwave warms food with electromagnetic energy. A refrigerator keeps food cool and slows spoilage.

A museum displays art, artifacts, or scientific collections. A hospital provides care for illness and injury. A school provides instruction in many subjects. A courthouse hosts trials and legal hearings.

A radio receives audio broadcasts through electronic signals. A telephone carries voices across long distances. A newspaper prints reports, opinions, and announcements. A mailbox collects letters for delivery routes.

A swing moves back and forth from overhead chains. A slide provides a smooth path toward the ground. A seesaw rises and falls around a central support. A sandbox holds loose sand for digging and play.
A cat often purrs during calm moments. A dog can learn commands and guard homes. A rabbit eats hay, greens, and pellets. A goldfish lives in fresh water inside an aquarium.
A giraffe has a very long neck for high leaves. A zebra has black and white body stripes. A rhinoceros has thick skin and powerful horns. A kangaroo moves with long powerful jumps.
A muffin is baked in a small paper cup. A doughnut is fried and often covered with sugar. A scone has a crumbly texture and mild sweetness. A tart has a shallow crust with filling.
A needle passes thread through fabric or cloth. A thimble protects a finger during sewing. A pin holds fabric pieces before stitching. A measuring tape shows length for patterns and hems.
A ring circles a finger as jewelry. A necklace hangs around the neck on a chain. A bracelet wraps around the wrist with a clasp. An earring attaches to an ear with a post or hook.
A map shows roads, rivers, and place names. A globe shows Earth on a round model. A compass shows direction with a magnetic needle. A legend explains symbols used on a map.
A keyboard enters letters and commands into a computer. A mouse moves a pointer across a screen. A printer puts digital text onto paper. A scanner converts paper images into digital files.

Chess is played on a board with sixteen pieces per side. Checkers is played with diagonal moves on dark squares. Scrabble uses letter tiles to build words. Backgammon combines dice rolls with strategic movement.
A sleeping bag provides warmth during nights outdoors. A compass helps hikers follow directions on trails. A cooler keeps food and drinks cold. A tent provides shelter in campsites and fields.
A violin has four strings and a wooden body. A trumpet makes sound through vibrating lips. A clarinet uses a single reed on the mouthpiece. A xylophone produces notes from struck wooden bars.
A pear has a narrow top and a broad base. A peach has soft skin and a central pit. A plum has smooth skin and juicy flesh. A cherry grows on stems in small pairs or clusters.
A beet grows underground and often shows deep red color. A leek has long pale stalks and green tops. A radish develops quickly in cool garden soil. A zucchini grows on a bushy summer squash plant.
A camel stores fat in a hump. A llama has thick wool and a calm nature. A yak lives in cold high mountain regions. A donkey carries loads with steady endurance.
A shrimp has a hard outer shell and many legs. A crab walks sideways with strong claws. A lobster has large pincers and a long body. A clam lives inside two joined shells.
A violin bow is strung with horsehair. A guitar pick helps pluck metal or nylon strings. A drumstick strikes cymbals and drumheads. A tuning fork produces a steady reference pitch.
A potholder protects hands from hot cookware. A ladle serves soup from deep pots. A peeler removes thin skin from vegetables. A rolling pin flattens dough for baking.
A daisy has white petals around a yellow center. A marigold blooms in bright orange or yellow colors. A violet often has small purple flowers. A peony can produce large layered blossoms.

A cedar tree has aromatic wood and evergreen needles. A birch tree often shows pale peeling bark. A spruce tree has pointed needles and hanging cones. A fig tree produces sweet fruit in warm climates.
A pelican carries fish in a large throat pouch. A hummingbird hovers near flowers for nectar. A raven has glossy feathers and a loud call. A flamingo often stands balanced in shallow water.
A whale shark is the largest living fish. A manta ray glides through water with broad fins. A squid moves by jet propulsion. An eel has a long body and smooth swimming motion.
A violin maker shapes wood for resonant instruments. A baker prepares bread and pastries for customers. A tailor alters clothing for better fit. A potter forms clay into bowls and vases.

A metro map simplifies stations and transfer lines. A road map shows highways and smaller routes. A topographic map uses contour lines for elevation. A weather map displays fronts, pressure, and precipitation.
A comet forms a glowing tail near the Sun. An asteroid orbits the Sun as a rocky body. A nebula contains gas and dust in space. A galaxy contains vast numbers of stars.
A ruler can be made from wood, metal, or plastic. A chalkboard accepts marks from dry chalk sticks. A globe can rotate on a small axis stand. A textbook presents organized information for study.

A violin concerto features a solo violin with orchestra. An opera combines singing, acting, and stage design. A march often uses a steady strong rhythm. A lullaby usually has a gentle calming character.
A maple leaf often has several pointed lobes. A fern frond uncurls from a tight spiral. A pine cone protects seeds beneath woody scales. A moss carpet can grow across damp stones.
A camel cricket has long legs and no wings. A firefly produces light through chemical reaction. A praying mantis holds folded forelegs near the head. A cicada makes loud sounds during warm seasons.
A crow can use tools in simple tasks. A pigeon can navigate over long distances. A gull often searches beaches and harbors for food. A swan has a long neck and strong wings.

A walnut tree produces nuts inside green husks. A chestnut tree drops glossy brown seeds in spiny cases. An olive tree can live for many decades. A lemon tree produces fragrant flowers and sour fruit.
A violin string vibrates faster at higher pitch. A drumhead vibrates after a strike from a stick. A flute tube resonates with moving air. A piano hammer strikes strings inside the instrument.
A seismograph records ground motion during earthquakes. A hygrometer measures moisture in the air. A scale measures weight or mass. A voltmeter measures electrical potential difference.
A cave forms underground in rock or ice. An island is surrounded by water on all sides. A peninsula extends into water while remaining connected to land. A valley lies between higher areas of land.
A calendar can hang on a wall or stand on a desk. An agenda lists tasks and appointments for days. A planner includes pages for dates and notes. A bulletin board displays papers with pins.
A blanket covers a bed for warmth. A pillow supports the head during sleep. A mattress cushions the body on a bed frame. A duvet is a soft quilt filled with insulating material.
A faucet controls water flow at a sink. A bathtub holds water for bathing. A mirror reflects light and visible images. A towel dries skin after washing.
